definitions.text: Preceding all others of a series or kind; the ordinal of one; earliest.
examples: The first day of September 2013 was a Sunday.

definitions.text: Most eminent or exalted; most excellent; chief; highest.
examples: Demosthenes was the first orator of Greece.

definitions.text: Of or belonging to a first family.
examples: First Cat; First Daughter; First Dog; First Son
Clean the sink first, before you even think of starting to cook.
I plunged nose first into the water.
I first witnessed a death when I was nine years old.

definitions.text: The person or thing in the first position.
examples: He was the first to complete the course.

definitions.text: The first gear of an engine.
Something that has never happened before; a new occurrence.
examples: This is a first. For once he has nothing to say.

definitions.text: To propose (a new motion) in a meeting, which must subsequently be seconded.
